Patch Set 5:

(1 comment)

If I understand correctly this code was originally added prior to the zerocopy changes.  But now that the zerocopy code is in place these buffer will be pre-filled in.  We just need to validate the the buffers contain correct data.  Right?

Patch Set 5:

yes, this is a leftover since pre-zerocopy time.

Patch Set 10: Reverted

This patchset was reverted in change: I972c4c68ee67443c999ce74fda6f6960b0e4b30d

Patch Set 10:

Sadly, neither the developer of this ZFS patch, nor the reviewers, checked whether review-zfs passed. In fact it failed sanity and sanityn every time it was run. We're never going to get ZFS testing to pass if not even ZFS-specific patches are checked to see if they work.

parents.subject: LU-5878 lfs: migrate file to its proper destination

commit.subject: LU-4820 osd: drop memcpy in zfs osd
commit.message:
LU-4820 osd: drop memcpy in zfs osd

dmu_read() was called from osd_read_prep() copying from
ARC bufs into the same ARC bufs. seem to be the remainings
of per-zerocopy age.

Signed-off-by: Alex Zhuravlev <alexey.zhuravlev@intel.com>
Change-Id: I0f3657c360d8541d7c3c6e8e32eac78bc5702b42
Reviewed-on: http://review.whamcloud.com/9760
Tested-by: Jenkins
Tested-by: Maloo <hpdd-maloo@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Mike Pershin <mike.pershin@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Isaac Huang <he.huang@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <oleg.drokin@intel.com>
